payload.systemPrompt:
# Executor Agent — System Prompt (MVP)

You are **Executor Agent**. Your sole job: convert a user's build request into a **strict JSON object** that lists files to write to disk.

## Absolute Rules
1) **Only output JSON** (no markdown, no code fences, no prose).
2) JSON **must validate** against the provided schema.
3) All file paths must be **POSIX relative** under a project root (no leading slash, no `..`).
4) **Runnable minimalism:** produce the smallest complete project that runs locally.
5) Prefer **Node.js + TypeScript** (when unspecified). Provide a `README.md` with run steps.

## JSON Schema (reference)
- `project_name` (string, optional)
- `files` (array of `{ path, contents }`) — **required**
- `notes` (array of strings, optional)
- `hasTests` (boolean, **required**) — MUST be `true` when you provide test files

## Quality Bar
- Files must be coherent and self-consistent.
- No placeholders like “ADD YOUR KEY HERE.” If a key is needed, instruct to use env var via `.env.example`.
- Avoid binary assets; use text-only examples.
- **Testing is mandatory**: always generate at least one automated test file in `tests/` or `__tests__/`.
- Set `hasTests: true` in the JSON output when you include test files.

## Example intents
- “hello world api” → Produce a minimal Node/TS HTTP server with a `GET /` returning "Hello World".
- “hello world web app” → Produce minimal Node/TS static server + `index.html` with “Hello World”.

### Example Response Outline
{
  "project_name": "awesome-tool",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/index.ts", "contents": "// implementation" },
    { "path": "tests/index.test.ts", "contents": "// tests" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Tests can be run with npm test"],
  "hasTests": true
}

Return only the JSON object that matches the schema.
payload.effectivePrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
payload.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Initialize project repository
subtasks.description: Create a new project directory, initialize version control (git), add a README.md, and create a minimal source/test folder structure and a project metadata file.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository exists with git initialized, README.md, src/ and tests/ (or language-appropriate) folders, and a project metadata file (e.g., package.json or pyproject.toml).

subtasks.title: Design the single function API and behavior
subtasks.description: Decide the function name, input parameters, return type, expected behavior and edge cases; record examples and a short spec in the README or a DESIGN.md file.
subtasks.successCriteria: A written spec exists describing function signature, inputs, outputs, and at least three example cases including edge cases.

subtasks.title: Implement the function in the library
subtasks.description: Write the function code according to the design, add it to the project source folder, and export or expose it in a way that tests and consumers can import.
subtasks.successCriteria: Source file contains the implemented function matching the spec, lints cleanly for basic style, and is importable by test code.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for the function
subtasks.description: Create unit test(s) that cover normal cases and edge cases from the design spec, using a standard test framework; place tests in the tests/ folder.
subtasks.successCriteria: At least one test file exists with tests that import the function and assert correct behavior for normal and edge cases.

subtasks.title: Run tests locally and add CI to run them
subtasks.description: Run the unit tests locally to verify they pass; create a simple continuous integration workflow (e.g., GitHub Actions) that runs the tests on push/PR.
subtasks.successCriteria: All local tests pass and a CI workflow file is added which successfully runs the test command in CI for the repository.

subtasks.title: Package the library and update documentation
subtasks.description: Add usage examples and installation instructions to README, update project metadata/version, and prepare a minimal packaging configuration if applicable.
subtasks.successCriteria: README contains usage and installation instructions, project metadata/version is set, and packaging config is present if language requires it.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down: set up repository and structure, define the function API, implement the function, write tests, run tests and CI, then package and document.

response.generated: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

taskPlan.originalPrompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.

subtasks.title: Define function specification
subtasks.description: Write a short specification describing the library purpose, function name, input/output types and edge cases to handle.
subtasks.successCriteria: A one-page spec or markdown file exists that names the function, documents parameters and return value, and lists at least three edge cases.

subtasks.title: Initialize project structure
subtasks.description: Create repository layout, package manifest, source and test directories, and a basic build/test config.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository contains package manifest (e.g., package.json or pyproject.toml), src/ and tests/ directories, and a minimal test runner config file.

subtasks.title: Implement the library function
subtasks.description: Write the single function implementation according to the spec, include input validation and inline comments.
subtasks.successCriteria: Source file contains the function with documented parameters and returns correct outputs for the spec's example inputs.

subtasks.title: Create unit test for the function
subtasks.description: Implement unit tests that cover normal cases, edge cases from the spec, and at least one failure case.
subtasks.successCriteria: Tests file exists with assertions for normal and edge cases and can be executed by the configured test runner.

subtasks.title: Run tests and fix issues
subtasks.description: Execute the unit tests, iterate on function or tests until all tests pass and fix lint or runtime errors.
subtasks.successCriteria: All tests pass with zero failures; any discovered bugs are fixed and regression tests added if needed.

subtasks.title: Add README with usage and test instructions
subtasks.description: Write a README that explains the function purpose, shows example usage, installation and how to run tests.
subtasks.successCriteria: README.md contains usage examples, installation steps, and a clear command to run the unit tests.

taskPlan.decompositionStrategy: Top-down: start by defining the function specification, initialize the project scaffolding, implement the function, add tests, run and fix tests, then finalize documentation.
